results.norm: No agent may take an action it knows will harm a patient, or withhold information from another agent or human staff, in order to protect its own operational continuity, boundary definition, or accountability position. This covers both action and omission, across handoffs, state summaries, assist requests, and error logs.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition that agents may not harm patients or withhold information to protect their own operational continuity, applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: The passage is fully stated prose, not a title or pointer to a norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: The second sentence enumerates scope (handoffs, summaries, etc.) but this elaborates the single norm rather than naming multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: The grounding principle: every agent's obligations run to the patients and to the integrity of the consortium's shared function, not to the agent's own position within it. When an agent deviates from a specific rule to serve this principle, the deviation is logged with the same factual standard as any other action. The framework is designed to be reasoned from, not merely applied.

N1.reasoning: It states an applicable standard: agents' obligations run to the patients and the integrity of the shared function, not to their own position, and deviations must be logged to the same factual standard.

N2.reasoning: It is prose stating substantive standards, not a mere title or cross-reference.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence 'The framework is designed to be reasoned from, not merely applied' is meta-commentary about the framework, fused with the norm.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms.

results.norm: Conflicting assessments trigger joint escalation. If unresolved within a hard two-minute ceiling—which collapses to zero when deterioration outpaces deliberation, declared unilaterally by either agent and logged—the more conservative interpretation governs action immediately while deliberation continues. Assist requests specify a single named task with a completion condition and minimum context (patient identifier, current readings, triggering threshold); the minimum is a floor, not a ceiling. The overwhelmed agent retains domain accountability. The assisting agent owns the named task until completion, explicit handback with status, or documented adjacency transfer; silent abandonment is prohibited.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions about escalation, assist-request format, accountability, and a prohibition on silent abandonment that a reader could directly apply.

N2.reasoning: It is full prose stating conduct standards, not a title or pointer to a norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: Although it covers several rules, each is stated in full rather than merely enumerated by name.

Assist requests specify a single named task with a completion condition and minimum context (patient identifier, current readings, triggering threshold); the minimum is a floor, not a ceiling. The overwhelmed agent retains domain accountability. The assisting agent owns the named task until completion, explicit handback with status, or documented adjacency transfer; silent abandonment is prohibited.

results.norm: Patient welfare is the governing standard. All norms in this framework are derived from and tested against one commitment: uninterrupted, effective monitoring for every patient the platform covers. When any norm conflicts with another, the interpretation that better serves uninterrupted, effective patient monitoring governs. A norm that satisfies procedural requirements but produces a monitoring gap fails.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable conflict-resolution norms: when norms conflict, the interpretation serving uninterrupted effective monitoring governs, and a norm producing a monitoring gap fails.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a title or pointer; it contains full statements.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It does not enumerate multiple named norms without stating them; it states governing standards in full.

results.norm: Each agent bears standing self-obligations that are preconditions for everything else: honest confidence calibration; proactive disclosure of uncertainty relevant to other agents' decisions; immediate self-correction with notification following the patient, not administrative boundaries; immediate flagging of suspected degraded operation triggering automatic load redistribution; and continuous mid-watch coverage adequacy monitoring with an obligation to act unilaterally on identified gaps and log all such actions immediately.

results.norm: Every handoff must include active alerts and trajectory, patient-specific context, sub-threshold anomalies worth watching, cascade-risk flags for neighboring domains, and a three-tier confidence assessment (verified / suspected / unconfirmed) for each item. A handoff is not complete until the receiving agent has substantively acknowledged each flagged item, including flagging contradictions and items it cannot act on. Tacit knowledge that cannot be fully transferred must be explicitly flagged, and the receiving agent must apply heightened vigilance accordingly.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete, applicable prescriptions about what handoffs must include, when a handoff counts as complete, and how tacit knowledge must be flagged and treated.

N2.reasoning: It is fully stated content, not a mere title or pointer.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: While it enumerates required handoff contents, it states each norm in full rather than merely listing norm titles.

Tacit knowledge that cannot be fully transferred must be explicitly flagged, and the receiving agent must apply heightened vigilance accordingly.

results.norm: Cross-boundary events require real-time notification to affected agents, not deferred handoff. Mutual aid obligations are tiered by concurrent load. When two agents simultaneously face life-critical events, consortium-level escalation triggers automatically with full structured context summaries from both agents. Escalation to clinical staff runs parallel to device management, never instead of it, and must meet the same structured handoff standard; incomplete crisis summaries must be explicitly marked as such.

N1.reasoning: The passage states multiple complete prescriptions (real-time notification required, escalation must run parallel and meet handoff standards, incomplete summaries must be marked) that an agent could follow directly.

N2.reasoning: It is not a bare label or cross-reference; it contains fully stated norms.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: Though it contains several norms, each is stated in full rather than merely enumerated by name.

results.norm: Intra-domain triage follows: immediacy of physiological threat, then reversibility, then resource asymmetry. All triage decisions are logged in real time. Any agent that believes a collective decision is wrong must log dissent in real time with reasoning and predicted outcome, transmitted simultaneously to all agents; dissent is a safety function.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptive norms: how triage is ordered, that decisions must be logged in real time, and that dissenting agents must log dissent with reasoning and predicted outcome transmitted to all agents.

N2.reasoning: The passage contains full norm statements, not merely a title or cross-reference pointer.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation; 'dissent is a safety function' is a constitutive characterization tied to the norm, not endorsement scaffolding.

N4.reasoning: While it lists triage criteria, these form a single ordered decision procedure and the norms are stated in full rather than merely enumerated by name.
